title: quercus parvula
intro: Quercus parvula, also known as the scrub oak, is a small evergreen tree or shrub that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant is rich in tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which give it medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus parvula include its ability to protect against heart disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. Quercus parvula is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
